hook.text: If you are having a scan for endometriosis, don't panic if it comes back normal Watch this
hook.emotional_mechanism_raw: Reassurance, reduces anxiety by normalizing negative scan results and countering fear
hook.target_audience_raw: Patients with endometriosis undergoing scans, especially those anxious about negative results

main_claim.text: A negative MRI does not mean endometriosis is absent; MRI findings often underestimate disease severity compared to laparoscopy, sometimes requiring a two-stage procedure

supporting_explanation.summary: MRI is good for assessing endometriosis above the rectal sigmoid, cecum, appendix, and pouch of Douglas, but in the speaker's experience, laparoscopic findings (gold standard) usually show worse disease. This lack of correlation means a negative scan does not clear you; you may need further investigation or a two-stage procedure with a colorectal surgeon

funnel_rationale: Broad educational content addressing a common myth about MRI reliability, raising awareness about diagnostic limitations

secondary_raw: Laparoscopy correlation, two-stage procedure, transvaginal ultrasound comparison

format_raw: TikTok video, talking head with onscreen text overlay and caption
